items.title: NAS JRB Fort Worth Modernizes Airfield Safety with New Precision Radar
items.description: NAS JRB Fort Worth, Texas — Naval Air Station (NAS) Joint Reserve Base (JRB) Fort Worth has taken a major step forward in aviation safety and operational readiness with the installation of a new AN/FPN-68A Precision Approach Radar. The modern solid-state system replaces the decades-old AN/FPN-63, delivering enhanced digital capabilities that will improve aircraft recovery in challenging Texas weather while significantly reducing maintenance demands.

items.title: Arkansas National Guard Legal Assistance Office Earns Army Chief of Staff Excellence Award
items.description: The Arkansas National Guard Office of Legal Assistance has earned the Fiscal Year 2025 Army Chief of Staff Award for Excellence in Legal Assistance, marking the tenth time the office has received the honor. The award recognizes the office’s continued commitment to serving Soldiers, retirees, disabled veterans and other eligible clients through legal support, improved accessibility and client-focused service. (U.S. Army National Guard Story by Civ. Brianna Young)

SANTA RITA, Guam — The U.S. Coast Guard Captain of the Port for Guam and the Northern Mariana Islands reset Port Heavy Weather Condition (PHWC) WHISKEY for the Port of Guam, effective 2 p.m. ChST on Thursday, July 9, reopening the port to 24/7 commercial traffic and cargo operations without waterway restrictions. All operators must coordinate operations with the Port of Guam and the harbormaster.

items.title: Headline: JTF-DC ensures safety during America's 250th Birthday
items.description: U.S. National Guardsmen and officers from multiple federal law enforcement agencies collaborate to provide security during Independence Day and Freedom 250 celebrations near the National Mall in Washington, July 4, 2026. Freedom 250 is a multi-event celebration marking the 250th anniversary of American Independence by bringing together civic, cultural, educational, and philanthropic partners to strengthen national unity and honor American history.

items.title: Illinois Army National Guard's 144th Army Band Gets Downpour of Support from Beecher, Illinois
items.description:
The farming community of Beecher, Illinois, doubles, even triples, in size every July 4 drawing people from throughout the region to celebrate our Nation's Independence in this slice of Americana on the prairie. The 250th Birthday of America was no different, except the rain, a lot of rain. 
But there was the Illinois Army National Guard's 144th Army Band marching and playing Patriotic music through the downpour.

items.title: The Legacy Continues: Retired Submariner Returns to Guam 50 Years Later
items.description: Fifty years after raising the national colors in Guam for America’s bicentennial, retired Navy Cmdr. Allan Riggs returned to the island July 4 to commemorate the nation’s 250th birthday alongside a new generation of submariners. Riggs has now participated in morning colors from Guam during both America’s 200th and 250th birthday celebrations, a distinction spanning half a century of submarine history.
